channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Eric Swalwell Sends Cease-and-Desist Letter to F.B.I. Over Case Files
item.description: A Trump administration effort to gather documents from an old investigation had alarmed law enforcement officials, who feared it would be used to smear the Democratic lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: An investigation of Representative Eric Swalwell of California over a decade ago into a Chinese fund-raiser suspected of spying brought no charges.

item.title: Senators Seek Answers From Trump Administration About Airport Immigration Arrests
item.description: Airport security officials have been sharing passenger data with immigration agents, but the program received little attention until videos captured a woman and her daughter being detained at San Francisco’s airport.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
item.media:description: The Transportation Security Administration began sharing passenger information with immigration officials a year ago, highlighting fliers who were on a list of people to be deported.

item.title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
item.description: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Anton Troianovski and Maria Abi-Habib
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An oil tanker near the coastline of Matanzas, Cuba, on Monday. The ban on foreign oil imports has plunged the country into a crisis.

item.title: DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Florida Airport for Trump
item.description: The airport near West Palm Beach will be rebranded the President Donald J. Trump International Airport on July 1, if approved by the Federal Aviation Administration.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., last month.

item.title: The Army Begins an Inquiry After Kid Rock Gets Apache Helicopter Flyby
item.description: The pro-Trump musician saluted the pair of Apache attack choppers, which appeared to be the same ones that flew low over a “No Kings” rally in Nashville.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: George Walker IV/Associated Press
item.media:description: A military helicopter flew over a “No Kings” protest on Saturday in Nashville.

item.title: A Crash Course on Primary Elections in Maine, Michigan and Beyond
item.description: Here’s a crash course on what’s coming up next.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Robert F. Bukaty/Associated Press; Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Democratic primary race for Senate in Maine is between Graham Platner and Gov. Janet Mills

item.title: A New U.S. Missile Hit a Sports Hall in Iran. Here’s What We Know About It.
item.description: Barely out of prototype testing, the Precision Strike Missile is shrouded in secrecy — including which Persian Gulf countries the Army is launching them from.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Mexico Pressures U.S. Over Deaths of Its Citizens in ICE Custody
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum, president of Mexico, said her country would take legal steps to demand better conditions at immigration detention facilities, where she said 14 Mexican citizens had died since President Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Jazmine Ulloa and Allison McCann
item.media:credit: Quetzalli Nicte-Ha/Reuters
item.media:description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico at a news conference on Monday.

item.title: Michigan Synagogue Attack Was ‘Inspired by Hezbollah,’ Officials Say
item.description: The man who rammed his vehicle into the synagogue was purposely targeting the Jewish community, officials said on Monday, detailing his days of planning.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, Mich.

item.title: Mark Sanford, Years After Scandal, Aims for a Comeback to Congress
item.description: The former South Carolina governor and congressman filed papers to run for his old seat, six years after running for president and nearly two decades after a high-profile affair.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Joshua Boucher/The State — Tribune News Service, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mark Sanford in 2023. His star has risen and fallen in Republican politics over the years.

item.title: Woman Spent Five Months in Jail After A.I. Linked Her to Bank Fraud Case
item.description: The police chief in Fargo, N.D., acknowledged “missteps” but stopped short of apologizing to Angela Lipps, a Tennessee resident who said she had never been to North Dakota before she was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:credit: Angela Lipps
item.media:description: Angela Lipps outside her home in Elizabethton, Tenn., before she was arrested in July 2025. She spent five months in jail in Tennessee and North Dakota before she was released on Dec. 24.

item.title: TSA Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks After Trump Signs Executive Order
item.description: A memo that President Trump signed on Friday ordering the Department of Homeland Security to pay T.S.A. officers did not specify whether they would be paid on a regular schedule.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.media:credit: Gene J. Puskar/Associated Press
item.media:description: Transportation Security Administration officers at a security checkpoint in Pittsburgh International Airport on Monday.

item.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
item.description: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Adam Goldman and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Many senior Iranian leaders and their deputies have been killed, and those who survive have had difficulty communicating.

item.title: Trump Administration Sues Minnesota Over Transgender Student Athletes
item.description: Last year, the state said it would not follow President Trump’s directive on transgender athlete participation in girls’ sports.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump signed an executive order last year barring transgender women from competing in women’s sports, and directed agencies to withdraw federal funding for any schools that refused to comply.

item.title: Student, 15, Shoots Texas High School Teacher
item.description: The teacher was taken to a hospital, and the student died at the scene, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan
item.category: Hill Country College Preparatory High School (Bulverde, Tex)

item.title: As Trump Squeezes Cuba, U.S. Military Exists in a Bubble
item.description: The Navy base at Guantánamo Bay sparkles at night while neighbors across a Cuban minefield struggle in darkness driven by a Trump fuel embargo.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Carol Rosenberg/The New York Times
item.media:description: A bowling alley at the Guantánamo Bay military base on a Saturday. Life on the base has been mostly unaffected by the energy crisis in Cuba.

item.title: Senate Republicans Make No Move to End DHS Shutdown in Brief Session
item.description: With Congress in recess, much of the Homeland Security Department remains without money because of the impasse on Capitol Hill, even as airport security workers are to begin getting paid.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: “If we had something good to go today, we could have done it today,” Senator John Hoeven said on Monday.

item.title: A Democratic Electrician Nabs a State Senate Seat in Republican Florida
item.description: With Brian Nathan’s victory certified, Democrats won two of three state legislative races in this month’s special elections, all in Republican-leaning districts.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Douglas R. Clifford/Tampa Bay Times, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Democratic candidate Brian Nathan, waving at potential voters, officially won a Florida state senate seat in a Republican district in West Tampa.

item.title: Marine Is Charged With Stealing Missile Launcher and Ammunition
item.description: A corporal is accused of taking weapons from his base, Camp Pendleton near San Diego, and reselling them.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Sandy Huffaker/Getty Images
item.media:description: Prosecutors said a Marine corporal stole and then sold weapons and ammunition from Camp Pendleton, which is near San Diego.

item.title: Justice Dept. Watchdog Has Gone Silent, Lawyers for Whistle-Blower Say
item.description: Lawyers for a fired Justice Department lawyer say the agency’s inspector general appears to have ignored at least 20 different requests to scrutinize misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lawyers for a Justice Department whistle-blower said that the department’s internal watchdog has seemingly ignored possible wrongdoing by the Trump administration.

item.title: How a Democratic Battle in Maine Is Challenging the Idea of Political Risk
item.description: Gov. Janet Mills argues that her rival for Senate, Graham Platner, could be doomed by his history of offensive online remarks. But at a time of anti-establishment anger, Mr. Platner says he is the safer choice.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Robert F. Bukaty/Associated Press; Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Democratic primary race for Senate in Maine is between Graham Platner, left, and Gov. Janet Mills, right.

item.title: What We Know About the T.S.A. and ICE Presence at Airports
item.description: Transportation Security Administration officers have begun getting paid during the partial government shutdown, but ICE agents may continue to work at airports.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: An ICE agent assisted passengers as they went through security at LaGuardia Airport in New York on Friday.

item.title: Markwayne Mullin Takes Over DHS With Less Flash but Same Mission
item.description: Markwayne Mullin, the incoming D.H.S. secretary, faces a difficult balance: a public reset of the agency while delivering on President Trump’s deportation agenda.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Markwayne Mullin being sworn in as homeland security secretary by Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday in the Oval Office. He has taken over a department that is in declining public repute, especially after the fatal shootings of two U.S. citizens in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: Trump’s Birthright Citizenship Order at Supreme Court Splits Conservative Scholars
item.description: Before President Trump’s order to limit birthright citizenship, there was widespread agreement that the 14th Amendment guaranteed citizenship for U.S.-born babies.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow

item.title: Federal Trial Over Deadly Heat in Texas Prisons Without Air-Conditioning Begins
item.description: Inmates in Texas prisons want a federal court to force the state to provide air-conditioning for all of its prison cells.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Verónica G. Cárdenas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inmates at the Reynaldo V. Lopez State Jail Facility in cells without air-conditioning amid extreme summer temperatures in Edinburg, Texas, in 2022.

item.title: Texas Border Wall Plan in Big Bend Draws Opposition From Republicans and Democrats
item.description: Plans for a wall in Big Bend National Park prompted public protests and behind-the-scenes lobbying to sway the Trump administration to stop.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman and Tamir Kalifa
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times

item.title: How a Civics School With a Conservative Bent Divided Its Supporters
item.description: A University of North Carolina program was intended to promote civil discourse and ideological diversity. Some of its early conservative supporters say it is doing the opposite.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Saul and Cornell Watson
item.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
item.media:description: The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill opened a new school focused on civics two years ago. It has drawn controversy almost since the beginning.
